# TESS Program G08030

Title: Tidal And Magnetic Interactions In Tight Eclipsing Binaries

PI: Martin, David - Tufts University

Type: SMALL

Summary: Stellar binaries are ubiquitous in the galaxy and give rise to some of the most heavily studied astrophysical phenomena, including supernovae and gravitational waves. Understanding the fundamental interactions within these systems is imperative. In this project, we focus on two key interactions: tidal and magnetic. Using TESS Cycle 8 photometry, we will measure starspot modulations their periodicity, phase, amplitude, and long-term variability over the now ~7-year TESS baseline. We also have access to archival radial velocity data spanning more than a decade, including two dozen Rossiter-McLaughlin measurements of spin-orbit obliquity. By combining these datasets, we aim to constrain the strength of tidal interactions and explore magnetic coupling between the stars, particularly in the for
